evals.prompt: Run the timesheet skill in --dry-run for 2026-04-13 only. Set DAILY_REPORTS_DIR=evals/files (relative to the skill root) so it reads the fixture meta dir and the fixture daily report. Print the planned-entries summary table and stop.
evals.expected_output: A markdown table covering 2026-04-13 with: two #265 commits collapsed into one ticket-265 entry on 'Island Health - Main', one ticket-266 entry on 'Island Health - Main', one no-ticket entry on 'Island Health - Main' for the merge commit, and one admin entry on the internal project. All hours should be 0.02. No Harvest POSTs and no tickets.json writes.
expectations: The summary table contains exactly one row for ticket #265 with both commit subjects bulleted in its Summary column
The summary table contains exactly one row for ticket #266 on the same date
The merge commit appears in a no-ticket row (Ticket column shows '(none)' or equivalent), not duplicated under a ticket bucket
The summary table includes one admin entry on the internal project with Ticket column '—'
Every Hours value in the summary table is 0.02
No call to mcp__harvest__harvest_create_time_entry is made
No file is written under evals/files/meta/tickets.json

evals.prompt: Run the timesheet skill in --dry-run for 2026-04-14 only, using DAILY_REPORTS_DIR=evals/files. Print the planned-entries summary table.
evals.expected_output: The cross-cutting entry with **Tickets:** #142, #143 should DUPLICATE — producing one 0.02h entry on Client Portal - API for #142 and another 0.02h entry on Client Portal - API for #143. The 11:20 commit referencing #143 collapses into the existing #143 bucket (same notes list grows to 2 items). The sideproject entry is silently skipped (excluded). No admin entry is created on a Tuesday — wait, Tuesday is a weekday, so an admin entry IS created.
expectations: The **Tickets:** line is treated as authoritative and produces two entries (#142 and #143), not split — each at 0.02h

The #143 entry's Summary column contains BOTH the 'Refactor session-token storage' bullet AND the 'Tighten validation on /api/auth/refresh' bullet (commits collapse across the structured-line and the body-regex source)
No entry is generated for the sideproject log entry (excluded project, silent skip)
An admin entry IS created for 2026-04-14 (Tuesday is a weekday)
The skipped sideproject entry is mentioned in the final 'skipped' summary list, not in the planned-entries table

evals.prompt: Run the timesheet skill in --dry-run for 2026-04-15 only, using DAILY_REPORTS_DIR=evals/files. The first log entry on that day has no ticket reference — when the skill prompts, REPLY '#268' to link it. Print the planned-entries summary table.
evals.expected_output: The 09:00 entry without a ticket prompts the user inline; given the reply '#268', it joins (or creates) a #268 bucket on Island Health - Main. The 14:00 #266 entry creates its own bucket. No backfill is written into the markdown source. Plus the Wednesday admin entry.
expectations: The skill prompts the user inline about the no-ticket 09:00 entry before printing the plan
After the user replies '#268', the planned-entries table contains a row for ticket #268 on Island Health - Main
The fixture markdown file evals/files/reports/2026-04-15-Wednesday.md is NOT modified (no **Tickets:** line is written back)
The planned-entries table contains a separate row for ticket #266

evals.prompt: Run the timesheet skill in --dry-run for 2026-04-18 only (Saturday), using DAILY_REPORTS_DIR=evals/files. Print the planned-entries summary table.
evals.expected_output: The Saturday hotfix #270 entry produces one ticket-270 row on Island Health - Main at 0.02h. Crucially, NO admin entry is generated (weekends skip the daily admin entry).
expectations: The planned-entries table contains exactly one row for ticket #270 on 2026-04-18
The planned-entries table does NOT contain an admin entry for 2026-04-18 (weekend rule)

evals.prompt: Verify sidecar upsert logic. Pre-seed evals/files/meta/tickets.json with an existing entry for 'ih#266' showing first_seen=2026-04-13, last_seen=2026-04-13, days_active=1, harvest_hours=0.02, harvest_entry_ids=[1001]. Then run the timesheet skill (NOT in dry-run) for 2026-04-15, using DAILY_REPORTS_DIR=evals/files. Mock the Harvest POST so it returns synthetic entry id 2002 for the new #266 entry on 2026-04-15. Verify how the sidecar gets updated.
evals.expected_output: After the run, tickets.json should show ih#266 with last_seen=2026-04-15, days_active=2, harvest_entry_ids=[1001, 2002] (de-duped, append-only set). first_seen stays at 2026-04-13. A new ih#268 key is created (from the linked-by-prompt entry, if the eval supplies that prompt) — or simply ih#266 alone is updated if #268 isn't part of this eval's scope.
expectations: tickets.json key ih#266 has last_seen='2026-04-15' after the run
tickets.json key ih#266 has days_active=2 after the run
tickets.json key ih#266 has harvest_entry_ids equal to [1001, 2002] in some order, with no duplicates
tickets.json key ih#266 has first_seen='2026-04-13' (unchanged)
The sidecar file is written atomically (a tickets.json.tmp must not be left behind on disk)
